CONTENT.International career: On 1 February 2009, he played at first senior level game for South Korea against Syria national football team.He was part of the South Korean team that won a bronze medal at the 2012 Summer Olympics.Kim appeared for South Korea in two qualifying matches for the 2014 FIFA World Cup. He was selected to the 23-man squad for the final tournament in Brazil, but did not appear in any matches as Lee Yong started all three of South Korea's matches at right-back.

SUMMARY: Kim Chang-soo (Korean: 김창수, Korean pronunciation: [kim.tɕʰaŋ.su]; born 12 September 1985) is a South Korean former footballer who currently plays as righy back.
